title: lupinus caudatus subsp. cutleri
intro: Lupinus caudatus subsp. cutleri, commonly known as Cutler's lupine, is a subspecies of the Lupinus caudatus plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cutler's lupine include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
